Class MainController

java.lang.Object
com.team62.controller.MainController
public class MainController extends Object
Controller class - handles business logic and coordinates between Model and View. This implementation backs the UI with the shared Postgres database.

  • Constructor Details

    • MainController

      public MainController()

  • Method Details

    • getAllMenuItems

      public List<MenuItem> getAllMenuItems()
      Returns:
      list of all menu items; dbId is set to item_id for update/delete operations.

    • addMenuItem

      public void addMenuItem(MenuItem item)
      Parameters:
      item - the menu item to add; must have name, category, and basePrice set; isActive can be set to false to hide from menu without deleting the caller (e.g. if name/category/price is null, or if a database error occurs during query execution)

    • updateMenuItem

      public void updateMenuItem(MenuItem item)
      Parameters:
      item - the menu item to update; must have dbId set to attempt update the caller (e.g. if dbId is null, or if a database error occurs during query execution)

    • deleteMenuItem

      public String deleteMenuItem(MenuItem item)
      Parameters:
      item - the menu item to delete; must have dbId set to attempt delete
      Returns:
      null on success, or an error message if delete failed (e.g. item is referenced by orders).

    • getAllInventoryItems

      public List<InventoryItem> getAllInventoryItems()
      Returns:
      list of all inventory items, with name and quantity; dbId is set to inventory_id for update/delete operations.

    • addInventoryItem

      public void addInventoryItem(InventoryItem item)
      Parameters:
      item - the inventory item to add; must have name and currentQuantity set, and the name must match an existing Item in the database the caller (e.g. if name is null, or if no matching Item is found, or if a database error occurs during query execution)

    • updateInventoryItem

      public void updateInventoryItem(InventoryItem item)
      Parameters:
      item - the inventory item to update; must have dbId set to attempt update the caller (e.g. if dbId is null, or if a database error occurs during query execution)

    • deleteInventoryItem

      public String deleteInventoryItem(InventoryItem item)
      Parameters:
      item - the inventory item to delete; must have dbId set to attempt delete
      Returns:
      null on success, or an error message if delete failed.

    • getAllEmployees

      public List<Employee> getAllEmployees()
      Returns:
      list of all employees; role and active status are parsed from the work_history JSONB field. If work_history is missing or malformed, defaults to role="" and active=true. On query error, returns an empty list and prints the stack trace.

    • addEmployee

      public void addEmployee(Employee employee)
      Parameters:
      employee - the employee to add; must have name and role set (role can be empty string for no role) the caller (e.g. if name is null, or if a database error occurs during query execution)

    • updateEmployee

      public void updateEmployee(Employee employee)
      Parameters:
      employee - the employee to update; must have dbId set to attempt update

    • deleteEmployee

      public String deleteEmployee(Employee employee)
      Parameters:
      employee - the employee to delete; must have dbId set to attempt delete
      Returns:
      null on success, or an error message if delete failed (e.g. employee is referenced by orders).

    • getTotalSalesForDate

      public BigDecimal getTotalSalesForDate(LocalDate date)
      Parameters:
      date - the date to calculate total sales for (time component ignored)
      Returns:
      the total sales amount for orders placed on the given date; returns 0 if no sales or if an error occurs during query execution

    • getOrderCountForDate

      public long getOrderCountForDate(LocalDate date)
      Parameters:
      date - the date to count orders for (time component ignored)
      Returns:
      the count of orders placed on the given date; returns 0 if none or if an error occurs

    • processOrder

      public String processOrder(SalesOrder order)
      Parameters:
      order - the order to process; must have at least one item with valid itemDbId and quantity > 0
      Returns:
      success or error message; on success, the order is persisted to the database and added to the in-memory list
